title: Withdraw ₳220,914 for Dolos: Sustaining a Lightweight Cardano Data Node

rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (enacted), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "This treasury withdrawal funds Dolos: Sustaining a Lightweight Cardano Data Node which will provide the following services: This request for support focuses on Dolos https://dolos.txpipe.io/ a lightweight Cardano data node, designed as a backend for dApps." though 8 review blocker(s) remain open. sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw ₳220,914 for Dolos: Sustaining a Lightweight Cardano Data Node
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: This treasury withdrawal funds Dolos: Sustaining a Lightweight Cardano Data Node which will provide the following services: This request for support focuses on Dolos https://dolos.txpipe.io/ a lightweight Cardano data node, designed as a backend for dApps., It is fine-tuned to solve a very narrow scope: keeping an updated copy of the ledger and replying to queries from trusted clients., Unlike a full Cardano node, Dolos is optimized for minimal resource usage while maintaining high data integrity and responsiveness., As an open-source project, Dolos has been steadily growing in adoption, with 366 commits, 367 pull requests, and contributions from 21 developers.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This treasury withdrawal funds Dolos: Sustaining a Lightweight Cardano Data Node which will provide the following services: This request for support focuses on Dolos https://dolos.txpipe.io/ a lightweight Cardano data node, designed as a backend for dApps.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): It is fine-tuned to solve a very narrow scope: keeping an updated copy of the ledger and replying to queries from trusted clients.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Unlike a full Cardano node, Dolos is optimized for minimal resource usage while maintaining high data integrity and responsiveness.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): As an open-source project, Dolos has been steadily growing in adoption, with 366 commits, 367 pull requests, and contributions from 21 developers.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): This proposal aims to solve the following problem: Dolos is a specialized Cardano data node designed to maintain an updated copy of the ledger and respond efficiently to queries.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Optimized for data integrity and speed, it provides developers with reliable blockchain data access while requiring a fraction of the resources of a full node.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
